channel.title: Comments on: Episode 38 – Monkshine Belgian Pale Ale
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: David Little
item.dc:creator: David Little
item.description:
Thanks Mikey for the clarification. Not so confusing ... arbitrary but understandable.
These came from my cellar as opposed to off the shelf so that may explain the ABV issue.
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Mikey for the clarification. Not so confusing … arbitrary but understandable.

These came from my cellar as opposed to off the shelf so that may explain the ABV issue.

item.title: By: Mikey
item.dc:creator: Mikey
item.description: I'm surprised this version of Monkshine is still on the market. Four+ amped up the ABV on this beer to 6.8% last October. And just to clarify, Utah considers anything over 4.0% abv to be "liquor". So any beer that's made above that is taxed as liquor and must be bottled. There is no 4.0% cap on the alcohol in beer, it just ceases to be beer in the eyes of the state. Confusing? I know....
item.content:encoded: I’m surprised this version of Monkshine is still on the market. Four+ amped up the ABV on this beer to 6.8% last October. And just to clarify, Utah considers anything over 4.0% abv to be “liquor”. So any beer that’s made above that is taxed as liquor and must be bottled. There is no 4.0% cap on the alcohol in beer, it just ceases to be beer in the eyes of the state. Confusing? I know….
